digest: Today's biggest story is the general availability of 1M context windows for Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6, generating significant community discussion on Hacker News. Claude Code also received major updates with MCP elicitation support and expanded 1M context access for paid plans. The community is actively building optimization tools, with a new prompt-caching solution promising 90% token savings.

stories.headline: 1M Context Window Now Generally Available for Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6
stories.summary: Anthropic has made 1M context windows generally available for Claude Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 models [1]. The announcement has generated significant community interest, with over 500 upvotes and 200+ comments on Hacker News [1].
key_points: Previously limited availability now expanded to general access
Strong community engagement suggests high demand for extended context capabilities

sources.title: 1M context is now generally available for Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6

stories.headline: Claude Opus 4.5 Released
stories.summary: Anthropic has released Claude Opus 4.5, though details about new features and improvements remain limited [1].

stories.headline: Claude Code v2.1.76 Introduces MCP Elicitation Support
stories.summary: The latest Claude Code update adds MCP elicitation support, allowing servers to request structured input mid-task via interactive dialogs [1]. The update also includes new CLI naming options and sparse checkout support for large monorepos [1].
key_points: MCP servers can now interrupt workflows to gather additional user input through forms or browser interfaces
New Elicitation and ElicitationResult hooks provide developer control over response handling
Sparse checkout support helps manage large codebases more efficiently

stories.headline: Claude Code v2.1.75 Expands 1M Context Access and Adds Session Management
stories.summary: Claude Code now provides 1M context window access for Opus 4.6 by default across Max, Team, and Enterprise plans, removing previous usage restrictions [1]. The update also introduces session customization features including color coding and improved memory file timestamps [1].
key_points: 1M context window previously required extra usage allocation, now included by default
New /color command and session naming help organize development workflows
Memory files now include timestamps to help Claude distinguish between fresh and stale information

stories.headline: New Tool Promises 90% Token Savings Through Automated Prompt Caching
stories.summary: A community-built tool called prompt-caching.ai automatically injects Anthropic cache breakpoints to optimize token usage [1]. The project has gained traction on Hacker News with developers discussing potential cost savings [1].
key_points: Automates the placement of cache breakpoints in prompts to maximize reuse
Claims to deliver up to 90% reduction in token costs for repetitive workflows

sources.title: Prompt-caching – auto-injects Anthropic cache breakpoints (90% token savings)

stories.headline: Use Claude's extended thinking for complex architectural decisions
stories.summary: When asking Claude to make non-trivial design choices — like choosing between patterns, structuring a new module, or planning a migration — enabling extended thinking produces significantly better results. Claude will reason through trade-offs before committing to an approach, catching issues that quick responses miss.
actionable_steps: For architecture questions, ask Claude to 'think through the trade-offs' explicitly
Use Claude Code's plan mode (shift+tab) for multi-step tasks that need careful reasoning
Review Claude's reasoning in extended thinking to catch flawed assumptions early

sources.title: Tips for getting better architectural advice from Claude

stories.headline: Write a CLAUDE.md file to set project conventions once
stories.summary: Adding a CLAUDE.md to your project root lets you define coding standards, test commands, and architectural decisions that persist across every Claude Code session. Think of it as onboarding documentation that Claude reads automatically — you teach it once instead of repeating yourself.
actionable_steps: Create a CLAUDE.md in your project root with build/test/lint commands
Add coding conventions like naming patterns, preferred libraries, and file structure
Include architectural decisions and constraints Claude should respect
Use /init in Claude Code to auto-generate a starter CLAUDE.md

stories.headline: Use @-mentions to give Claude Code precise file context
stories.summary: Boris Cherny, creator of Claude Code, recommends using @-mentions to point Claude at specific files instead of letting it search. This dramatically reduces token usage and improves accuracy since Claude works with the exact files you intend rather than guessing from project structure.
actionable_steps: Type @ in Claude Code to see a file picker with fuzzy search
Select the specific files relevant to your task before writing your prompt
Combine multiple @-mentions for cross-file tasks like refactoring
